news_items.title: US won't start players on yellow cards against Turkey, says Pochettino | The Straits Times
news_items.text:
US won't start players on yellow cards against Turkey, says Pochettino | The Straits Times

myST+

Menu

# US won't start players on yellow cards against Turkey, says Pochettino

Sign up now: Get the biggest sports news in your inbox

Published Jun 25, 2026, 09:41 AM

Updated Jun 25, 2026, 02:38 PM

Set as preferred source

Listen

INGLEWOOD, California - United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said he won't start any of his already-qualified team's four players who have yellow cards when they face Turkey in the World Cup clash on June 25.

The US have already guaranteed top spot in Group D, and with Turkey already out, the match is a dead rubber. However, Pochettino said the players he picks need the hunger to win.

Although he has not made a final decision on whether to play forward Christian Pulisic, whoever he selects must have a desire to "eat the grass," he told reporters on June 24.

Argentine Pochettino has decided not to start with the four players on bookings – defender Chris Richards, wing back Antonee Robinson, midfielder Tyler Adams and forward Folarin Balogun – which means they are unlikely to feature.

The yellow cards are a problem not just because another booking w

news_items.title: The Four USMNT Players Walking a Suspension Tightrope vs. Turkiye
news_items.text:
The Four USMNT Players Walking a Suspension Tightrope vs. Turkiye

The U.S. men’s national team already clinched its spot in the knockout rounds, but there is still some cause for concern when the Stars and Stripes clash with Türkiye on Thursday.

Four of Mauricio Pochettino’s most important players—Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, Chris Richards and Antonee Robinson—are all one yellow card away from suspension. Should any of the four players enter the referee’s book at SoFi Stadium, they will be suspended for the round of 32.

Adams, who has played every second of the 2026 World Cup so far, was cautioned in the team’s 4–1 win over Paraguay to kick off the tournament. Tim Ream also received a yellow card in the lopsided match, but a quick VAR review saw the booking rescinded.

The USMNT then defeated Australia 2–0 to punch its ticket to the knockout stage and ultimately clinch the top spot in Group D. The game was a physical and hostile affair, with seven players picking up yellow cards before the final whistle sounded in Seattle. On the host’s side, the guilty parties were Balogun, Richards and Robinson.

Now the four Americans are one caution away from missing out on the biggest gam

Four of Mauricio Pochettino’s most important players—Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, Chris Richards and Antonee Robinson—are all one yellow card away from suspension. Should any of the four players enter the referee’s book at SoFi Stadium, they will be suspended for the round of 32.

Adams, who has played every second of the 2026 World Cup so far, was cautioned in the team’s 4–1 win over Paraguay to kick off the tournament. Tim Ream also received a yellow card in the lopsided match, but a quick VAR review saw the booking rescinded.

The USMNT then defeated Australia 2–0 to punch its ticket to the knockout stage and ultimately clinch the top spot in Group D. The game was a physical and hostile affair, with seven players picking up yellow cards before the final whistle sounded in Seattle. On the host’s side, the guilty parties were Balogun, Richards and Robinson.

news_items.author: Zach Cavanagh Thu, June 25, 2026 at 10:50 AM UTC · 6 min read
news_items.text:
USMNT Notebook: Yellow cards force roster changes for 'dead rubber' match vs. Türkiye - Yahoo Sports

### Hornets reportedly trading LaMelo Ball to Timberwolves

NBA Draft Round 2 recap

Story by

#3 Chris Richards and #16 Alex Freeman of the United States high five during a FIFA World Cup match against Australia, Friday June 19th, 2026 in Seattle, Washington.

(Nico Alba - The Sporting Tribune)

IRVINE, Calif. – This is not a normal set of circumstances for the United States men’s national team in a FIFA World Cup group stage finale.

For just the third time in its history, the USMNT has already won its group. Heck, it’s just the first time the U.S. has won consecutive games in a World Cup group stage since 1930–there were only two group stage games then–and only the third time the men’s team has won two games in a single World Cup.

Advertisement

Advertisement

# US won't start players on yellow cards against Turkey, says Pochettino

Sign up now: Get the biggest sports news in your inbox

Published Jun 25, 2026, 09:41 AM

Updated Jun 25, 2026, 02:38 PM

Set as preferred source

Listen

INGLEWOOD, California - United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said he won't start any of his already-qualified team's four players who have yellow cards when they face Turkey in the World Cup clash on June 25.

The US have already guaranteed top spot in Group D, and with Turkey already out, the match is a dead rubber. However, Pochettino said the players he picks need the hunger to win.

Although he has not made a final decision on whether to play forward Christian Pulisic, whoever he selects must have a desire to "eat the grass," he told reporters on June 24.

Argentine Pochettino has decided not to start with the four players on bookings – defender Chris Richards, wing back Antonee Robinson, midfielder Tyler Adams and forward Folarin Balogun – which means they are unlikely to feature.

The yellow cards are a problem not just because another booking w"}
{"title": "The Four USMNT Players Walking a Suspension Tightrope vs. Turkiye", "url": "https://www.si.com/soccer/four-usmnt-players-suspension-tightrope-vs-turkiye", "publishedDate": "2026-06-25T01:00:00.000Z", "author": null, "text": "The Four USMNT Players Walking a Suspension Tightrope vs. Turkiye

The U.S. men’s national team already clinched its spot in the knockout rounds, but there is still some cause for concern when the Stars and Stripes clash with Türkiye on Thursday.

Four of Mauricio Pochettino’s most important players—Tyler Adams, Folarin Balogun, Chris Richards and Antonee Robinson—are all one yellow card away from suspension. Should any of the four players enter the referee’s book at SoFi Stadium, they will be suspended for the round of 32.

Adams, who has played every second of the 2026 World Cup so far, was cautioned in the team’s 4–1 win over Paraguay to kick off the tournament. Tim Ream also received a yellow card in the lopsided match, but a quick VAR review saw the booking rescinded.

The USMNT then defeated Australia 2–0 to punch its ticket to the knockout stage and ultimately clinch the top spot in Group D. The game was a physical and hostile affair, with seven players picking up yellow cards before the final whistle sounded in Seattle. On the host’s side, the guilty parties were Balogun, Richards and Robinson.

Now the four Americans are one caution away from missing out on the biggest gam"}

#3 Chris Richards and #16 Alex Freeman of the United States high five during a FIFA World Cup match against Australia, Friday June 19th, 2026 in Seattle, Washington.

(Nico Alba - The Sporting Tribune)

IRVINE, Calif. – This is not a normal set of circumstances for the United States men’s national team in a FIFA World Cup group stage finale.

For just the third time in its history, the USMNT has already won its group. Heck, it’s just the first time the U.S. has won consecutive games in a World Cup group stage since 1930–there were only two group stage games then–and only the third time the men’s team has won two games in a single World Cup.
